title: Contributor signal (Phase 8 crowdsourced taxonomy)
description: One rating of how much a single trait dimension matters for a single archetype, submitted by a verified role-holder or hiring manager. This is the v2 crowdsourcing input described in PLAN.md Phase 8: role-holders and managers are kept as SEPARATE signals (contributor_role) so the gap between what a role demands day-to-day and what managers screen for is itself measurable. Each line in data/contributor-signals.jsonl is one object of this shape. Signals are raw, unaggregated input — the expert-seeded taxonomy in taxonomy/archetypes.json is never mutated by a single submission; aggregation with minimum-n thresholds, outlier damping, and audit history is the separate Phase 8 aggregation model (not yet built).

archetype_id.description: Stable id of the archetype being rated. Must match an id in taxonomy/archetypes.json (e.g. "sales-engineer").

dimension_id.description: Stable id of the trait dimension being rated for this archetype. Must match an id in taxonomy/dimensions.json (e.g. "client_facing_comfort").

contributor_role.description: Which side of the market the contributor is on. Role-holders rate what the role actually demands day-to-day; hiring managers rate what they screen/hire for. Kept as separate signals on purpose — the gap between the two is a publishable per-role insight.

signal_value.description: How much this dimension matters for this archetype, on the same 1-5 behavioral-anchor scale the expert taxonomy uses. 1 = barely relevant, 5 = defining.

verified_title.description: The contributor's current job title as verified (Phase 8 target: LinkedIn OAuth verifies the stated current title only). Null/absent until a verification provider is wired up; v1 collection is self-reported and should be treated as unverified.

years_in_role.description: Self-reported years in this kind of role. Combined with verified_title to weight a signal's credibility; not a verification by itself.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp stamped server-side at submission (not client-supplied), so ordering and rate-analysis don't depend on client clocks.

session_id.description: Opaque per-visit id (client-generated UUID) linking the several dimension signals submitted together in one 'Rate this role' interaction. Not tied to any account or personal identity — no login exists in v1/v2 collection. Enables per-session dedup and outlier detection during aggregation.
